intro: Moschopsis rosulata, also known as Moschopsis, is a plant species belonging to the family Cunoniaceae, native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it a potential treatment for various health issues such as wound healing, skin infections, and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Moschopsis rosulata include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
